text:
If you’ve been wondering why some athletes are choosing to wear all black at the ParisOlympics, then wonder no more.

It’s all down to Bandit Running, who are “making waves” with their subtle outfits which actually have a deeper meaning.

@JakeHeyenexplained on YouTube Shorts that often, athletes will “dress head to toe in branded gear” at Olympic trials – but the athletes choosing all black are doing so because of Bandit Running “and their campaign, the Unsponsored Project."

He said that at the trials, the athletes are not only running for their Olympic spot but also “future sponsorship."

Jake explained the athletes could go out and “rep regular gear with logos on it”, but it makes more sense for them to not do that, as it gives the brands free publicity for no reason.

“When they make history, who reaps a big reward for free?”, Jake asked, revealing it would be the brands that made money off the athlete winning if they were sporting their gear.

“So Bandit made gear for these athletes to wear that is completely unbranded. But the purpose isn’t to stick it to these big brands.

“It’s to empower these athletes to declare their independence, and to be a message to brands like, ‘Hey, I’m unsponsored, I just made history, who wants me now?”

He said this leads to “money in the pockets of these athletes from future deals, and exposure for Bandit, despite not having their logo plastered on this gear."

He described it as a “win-win” situation for all involved.

In the comments, people praised the brand, saying: “This is one of the coolest things ever.

“It’s rare you see a company take the time and money to give back to the sport and the people that make the sport popular. Wish we had more companies like this”.

Another said: “Someone in their marketing department needs a raise”.

Someone else wrote: “That is so legit! This company is absolutely legendary and I feel like all aspiring Olympic and professional athletes should be made aware of it.”

“Such a clever solution to a problem and an ingenious way to provide a service to your customer that still makes you money in the least sleazy way I've seen in years”, a fan penned.

title: 'Genius' reason some Olympians wear all black when competing | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Danielle Kate Wroe
description: If you see an athlete running in all black, unbranded gear, there's a ‘genius' reason behind it - especially if they're successful.
